title: cicendia campestris
intro: Cicendia campestris, also known as marsh marigold or kingcup, is a perennial plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cicendia campestris include reducing symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin irritations, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its roots and flowers can be used to make salves and ointments, making it a valuable natural remedy for various skin issues.
